text.title: BA Activities in Scrum
text.summary: BA activities per Scrum phase, Sprint 0 responsibilities, and ad-hoc clarification response-time target.

rule.statement: BA activities must be distributed across all Scrum phases, not concentrated in Sprint 0. Sprint 0 establishes vision, roadmap, and initial backlog; all subsequent ceremonies require BA participation to maintain quality.
rule.rationale: BAs who disappear after Sprint 0 produce an initial backlog that degrades as development teams interpret stories without expert BA support during execution.

list.item: Sprint 0: vision and roadmap facilitation, initial backlog creation from stakeholder sessions, acceptance criteria on the first 5-10 stories
Backlog Refinement: user story elaboration, acceptance criteria authoring, models and wireframe review, dependency identification with other teams
Sprint Planning: story clarification, dependency flagging, documenting sprint commitments with acceptance criteria confirmed
Sprint Execution: ad-hoc clarification (target response under 30 minutes), prototype review, preparing next sprint backlog items
Sprint Review: validate acceptance criteria are met, capture stakeholder feedback, update backlog with feedback-driven changes
Retrospective: share BA process improvement observations, identify collaboration gaps between BA and developers or QA

rule.statement: Respond to in-sprint developer or QA clarification requests within 30 minutes during working hours. Unresolved clarifications block development and cause sprint failure or unreviewed implementation decisions.
rule.rationale: Blocked developers make implementation decisions unilaterally; a 30-minute response target prevents this without requiring the BA to be on-call continuously.
